sanskrit:
परस्तस्मात्तु भावोऽन्योऽव्यक्तोऽव्यक्तात्सनातनः।
यः स सर्वेषु भूतेषु नश्यत्सु न विनश्यति॥
transliteration: paras tasmāt tu bhāvo ’nyo ’vyakto ’vyaktāt sanātanaḥ / yaḥ sa sarveṣu bhūteṣu naśyatsu na vinaśyati
literal_meaning: Beyond that unmanifest state there is another, unmanifest and eternal, which does not perish when all beings perish.
contextual_meaning: Beneath every transient identity, mood, or life-situation lies a changeless awareness that survives the collapse of any story you tell about yourself.
psychological_state: The moment when a client realizes, 'I am not my diagnosis, my trauma, or even my successes; I am the observing presence that remains after each chapter ends.'
trigger_scenario: A person who has lost job, relationship, and health in one year sits in therapy, finally glimpsing that something alive in them is untouched by the wreckage.
anchor_text: Rest as the quiet awareness that outlives every story you tell about yourself.
